title: Allow manual time tracking by entering hours and minutes without having to enter a specific start and end time
excerpt: Allow manual time tracking by entering hours and minutes without having to enter a specific start and end time

posts.author: josephbullock (Joseph Bullock)
posts.cookedHtml: Allow manual time tracking by entering hours and minutes without having to enter a specific start and end time

posts.author: PMPaige (Paige Lunny)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be incredibly helpful. My team needs to track total hours per week, not by day. If you worked 10 hours on a certain project that week, it would be nice to simply input 10 instead of doing 2 hours per day. Currently I’m using a numbers column as a work around. I’ve set up the project to be at the group level, each week is a task, and each person working is a subtask. Individuals go in each week and input their total hours worked for the week in the number column. Then, I adjust the settings of the subtask column to “Show Summary on Parent Item.” That provides me a sum of hours worked that week. Unfortunately, this workaround does not include the other features of a true time tracking column.

posts.author: MistyMK (AppBay)
posts.cookedHtml:
@PMPaige @josephbullock
You can now easily update a time entry with one click using Smart Timer and Team Tracking app which has the same look and feel as the native monday.com timer.

We had the same pain point with our service based business, sometimes there was a flat rate of time or the staff accidentally recorded the wrong time and needed to easily update it, so we got our dev to build a solution which we have now released to the monday.com marketplace.

We also built in the ability for the team to see LIVE who is recording time on what, this helps us to manage our remote team and re-delegate work as needed.

We have only recently launched so if there are any tweaks that would help it better suit your business feel free to add to the live chat and we will see if we can roll it out in the next update.
